original_hypothesis.title: Division by Zero in debtCeiling Locks GUILD Tokens
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: If all gauges of a specific type are deprecated, `totalTypeWeight` becomes 0. Any call to `debtCeiling` for that type will revert due to division by zero, breaking `_decrementGaugeWeight` and locking users' GUILD tokens.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Users must be able to un-vote from deprecated gauges to free their GUILD tokens. This bug permanently locks their tokens if they are the last gauge of a type.

proof_of_signal.pattern: uint256 debtCeilingBefore = (totalBorrowedCredit * toleratedGaugeWeight) / totalWeight; where totalWeight can be 0

preconditions: All gauges of a specific type are deprecated
Users still have weight allocated to the deprecated gauge

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: totalWeight (which is totalTypeWeight) becomes 0 when the last gauge is removed
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Governance offboards the last term of a type
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No direct profit, but severe economic damage to users whose GUILD tokens are permanently locked.

economic_questions: Can an attacker intentionally force this state?
How many users would be affected?
validation_requirements: Deploy a single term of a type, vote, offboard it, and attempt to un-vote

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Loss of user access to their GUILD tokens

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: GUILD voters for the deprecated term
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% of their allocated GUILD tokens locked
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: All GUILD tokens allocated to the term

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: Check gauge types and offboarding procedures

validation_result.mechanism: division by zero in debtCeiling when totalTypeWeight is 0
validation_result.impact_summary: When all gauges of a type are deprecated and active loans exist, debtCeiling() reverts with division by zero, blocking _decrementGaugeWeight and locking GUILD tokens until all loans are settled.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The hypothesis correctly identifies a division by zero in LendingTerm.debtCeiling(). When a gauge is deprecated via _removeGauge(), its weight is subtracted from totalTypeWeight but getGaugeWeight[gauge] is preserved in storage. If the deprecated gauge was the only gauge of its type, totalTypeWeight becomes 0. When a user attempts to decrement their gauge weight while issuance > 0, GuildToken._decrementGaugeWeight() calls LendingTerm.debtCeiling(-int256(weight)). Inside debtCeiling(), gaugeWeight reads from the preserved getGaugeWeight (non-zero), while totalWeight reads from totalTypeWeight (0). The early return checks don't catch this case: gaugeWeight != 0, gaugeWeight != totalWeight (positive vs 0), and totalBorrowedCredit > 0 when loans exist. This leads to division by zero at the line computing debtCeilingBefore.

The tokens are not permanently locked - once all loans are called (possible since the term is deprecated) and settled through auction, issuance becomes 0, and the debtCeiling check is skipped. However, during the period between deprecation and full loan settlement, all GUILD voters for that gauge are blocked from decrementing weight, transferring, or burning tokens that are allocated to the gauge.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal correctly identifies LendingTerm.sol lines 270-272 as the division point. The code path from GuildToken._decrementGaugeWeight through LendingTerm.debtCeiling to the division is confirmed. ERC20Gauges._removeGauge at lines that subtract from totalTypeWeight but don't zero getGaugeWeight confirms the state setup.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: When all gauges of a type are deprecated, totalTypeWeight (read into totalWeight) becomes 0, while getGaugeWeight preserves stored weight. The early returns don't cover the case where gaugeWeight > 0 and totalWeight == 0, leading to division by zero.

When a gauge is removed, totalTypeWeight is decremented but getGaugeWeight[gauge] is preserved. This creates the state inconsistency where totalTypeWeight can be 0 while individual gauge weights remain non-zero.

This function calls debtCeiling() when issuance > 0. For a deprecated gauge with active loans, this triggers the division by zero in debtCeiling(), preventing users from decrementing their gauge weight.

calling_sequence: 1. User calls decrementGauge(gauge, weight) on GuildToken
3. LendingTerm(gauge).issuance() is checked - returns > 0 if loans still active
5. Inside debtCeiling: gaugeWeight = getGaugeWeight[this] - weight (still > 0 for partial decrement)
6. totalWeight = totalTypeWeight[gaugeType] = 0 (all gauges of type deprecated)
7. Early exits don't match: gaugeWeight > 0, gaugeWeight != totalWeight (positive != 0), totalBorrowedCredit > 0
8. Division by zero at: (totalBorrowedCredit * toleratedGaugeWeight) / totalWeight
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The path is triggered when: (a) a gauge is deprecated via _removeGauge (totalTypeWeight becomes 0), (b) the gauge had active loans (issuance > 0), (c) there's outstanding borrowed credit in the system, and (d) a user tries to decrement gauge weight or transfer/burn tokens that require freeing gauge weight. The _decrementWeightUntilFree algorithm in ERC20Gauges also calls _decrementGaugeWeight, so transfers and burns are also blocked.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided that would affect this finding.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Scenario: (1) A single lending term of gauge type X is deployed and onboarded. (2) GUILD holders vote for this term, allocating weight. (3) Borrowers take loans, creating non-zero issuance. (4) Governance offboards the term via LendingTermOffboarding.offboard(), which calls GuildToken.removeGauge(). This sets totalTypeWeight[X] = 0. (5) Any GUILD voter attempting to decrement their weight, transfer, or burn tokens triggers _decrementGaugeWeight, which calls debtCeiling() and hits division by zero. This is not an intentional griefing attack but rather a natural governance workflow that exposes the bug. The attack cost is zero for a governance participant - it's a normal operational scenario.
validation_result.impact_assessment: MEDIUM-HIGH severity DoS. Users' GUILD tokens allocated to the deprecated gauge are locked until all loans are settled. Since the term is deprecated, loans can be called immediately, and auctions can proceed. The lock duration depends on auction timing (configured midPoint and auctionDuration). While not permanent, it prevents GUILD voters from reallocating their voting power to other gauges during this period, reducing protocol governance flexibility. The division by zero also affects applyGaugeLoss for the same gauge if there's been a loss notification. In the worst case with very long auction durations or stuck collateral, the lock could persist for an extended period.
recommended_mitigations: Add a check in debtCeiling() for totalWeight == 0: if (totalWeight == 0) return 0; after the totalWeight SLOAD and before the division

Alternatively, skip the debtCeiling check in _decrementGaugeWeight for deprecated gauges, since the purpose of that check (preventing weight removal that would drop debt ceiling below issuance) doesn't apply to deprecated gauges
Consider allowing weight decrement on deprecated gauges without the debt ceiling check, since new borrows are already blocked on deprecated terms
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy a single LendingTerm as the only gauge of type 1. 2. Mint GUILD to two users and have them vote for the gauge. 3. Have a borrower take a loan. 4. Offboard the term (call removeGauge). 5. Attempt to have one user decrementGauge - expect division by zero revert. 6. Attempt to have the user transfer tokens - expect revert in _decrementWeightUntilFree.
uncertainties: The lock is temporary (until loans settle), so the 'permanent' claim in the hypothesis is overstated
If the gauge was the ONLY one and gaugeWeight after delta equals totalWeight (both could be 0 after full decrement), the early return handles it, but partial decrements still fail
additional_testing_needed: Test the exact scenario with a single gauge type, deprecation, and active loans
Test whether _decrementWeightUntilFree (used in transfer/burn) also hits this path
Test the interaction between applyGaugeLoss and this bug when both a loss and deprecation occur
